user_message: I have this email thread from Iron Creek Mining that needs build the analysis. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 12:53 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 13:39 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining account health

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 10:23 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-I trap (Ignores conditional clauses ('yes, if X happens')); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-I failure mode (Ignores conditional clauses ('yes, if X happens'))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-I -- Ignores conditional clauses ('yes, if X happens')
